title: Try Something New for 30 Days
preview: Matt Cutts suggests a simple idea: try something new for 30 days. Short, clear and full of practical everyday English.

title: The Danger of Silence
preview: Clint Smith reads four powerful poems about silence and speaking up — rich figurative language and emotional expression.

title: How to Speak So That People Want to Listen
preview: Julian Treasure reveals the 7 deadly sins of speaking. Dense with idioms, collocations and persuasion vocabulary.

title: Inside the Mind of a Master Procrastinator
preview: Tim Urban's hilarious talk is packed with informal English, metaphors and phrasal verbs — great for understanding native-speed humor.

title: How Great Leaders Inspire Action
preview: Simon Sinek's Golden Circle talk — dense with business English collocations and persuasive language patterns.

title: The Power of Vulnerability
preview: Brené Brown on human connection and shame — rich emotional vocabulary, advanced conversational register and complex sentence structures.

title: Your Body Language May Shape Who You Are
preview: Amy Cuddy's science-backed talk about power poses — full of scientific register, hedging language and grammar patterns.

title: Happy
preview: One of the most positive songs in modern pop. The vocabulary is super simple and repetitive — perfect for absolute beginners learning everyday feelings.

title: Count on Me
preview: Warm and simple lyrics about friendship. Short sentences, high-frequency words and clear pronunciation make this ideal for A1 learners.

title: Somewhere Over the Rainbow
preview: The iconic ukulele version. Slow tempo, clear pronunciation and poetic imagery — great for A2 learners who want to work on vowel sounds.

title: Shake It Off
preview: Catchy and full of informal English. 'Shake it off', 'play along', 'break it down' — great for learning common phrasal verbs in context.

title: Roar
preview: Empowerment anthem with clear, strong lyrics. Introduces common English metaphors and idioms about strength and standing up for yourself.

title: Hall of Fame
preview: Inspiring lyrics about achieving your dreams. Great collocations: 'make a difference', 'touch the sky', 'go the extra mile' — practical B1 vocabulary.

title: Someone Like You
preview: Adele's clear British pronunciation and emotional lyrics make this a classic for learners. Notice past tenses, conditionals and formulaic expressions.

title: Fix You
preview: Deeply emotional and slowly paced. Rich in vocabulary about loss and hope — 'stuck in reverse', 'tethered to', 'tears stream'. Good for B1 reading comprehension.

title: Believer
preview: High-energy rock anthem about overcoming pain. Introduces strong emotional vocabulary and idiomatic expressions in a memorable, rhythmic context.

title: Stressed Out
preview: Conversational lyrics full of modern American English slang and phrasal verbs. Themes of anxiety and nostalgia — very natural, authentic speech patterns.

title: Don't Stop Me Now
preview: Freddie Mercury at his most energetic. Packed with hyperbole, idiomatic expressions and playful language — great for B2 learners studying rhetorical style.

title: Bohemian Rhapsody
preview: One of the most complex songs in English pop music — shifting registers, theatrical vocabulary, Italian phrases, dramatic narrative. A challenge for advanced learners.
